title: Charred Algerian Tomatoes Flatbread
description: A flavorful flatbread dish featuring charred, crisp Algerian tomatoes and double-rise-bread, perfect for a low-carb friendly meal. This monsoon-inspired dish is a perfect blend of textures and tastes
instructions: Cut the double-rise bread into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes.
Brush the bread cubes with olive oil and season with garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Place the bread cubes on a baking sheet and cook for 10-12 minutes or until lightly toasted, rotating halfway through.
While the bread is cooking, heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Brush the Algerian tomatoes with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the tomatoes for 2-3 minutes per side or until charred and crispy.
To assemble the flatbread, place a spoonful of grilled tomatoes on top of the toasted bread cubes.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
